results.title: Bihar invites influencers to showcase Shravani Mela, announces Rs 3 lakh prize
results.description: New Delhi: The Bihar tourism department has announced a state-wide video-making competition for content creators covering the upcoming Shravani Mela, offering a top prize of Rs 3 lakh. Titled 'Shravani Mela 2026: Through an Influencer's Lens', the contest is open to Indian citizens aged 18 years and above who have a combined following of at least 25,000 across Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube. Capture the spirit of Shravani Mela 2026 and win cash prizes up to ₹3,00,000! Inviting all content creators, bloggers, influencers, photographers, videographers, and reel makers . Register now at https://t.co/wLWSQpNkxu and showcase your creativity. #ShravaniMela2026 ... pic.twitter.com/0U9wMhCOn5 — Bihar Tourism (@TourismBiharGov) July 17, 2026 Participants are required to create reels or short videos showcasing the Shravani Mela, which will be held from July 30 to August 28 along the Kanwariya Path spanning Munger, Banka and Bhagalpur districts. Tourism Secretary Lokesh Kumar Singh said the initiative is intended to encourage creators to present the fair's cultural and spiritual significance to a wider online audience. The winner will receive Rs 3 lakh, while the second- and third-place entries will be awarded Rs 2 lakh and Rs 1 lakh, respectively. Two fourth-place winners will receive Rs 50,000 each, while five consolation prizes of Rs 25,000 each have also been announced. According to the competition guidelines, videos must be between 30 seconds and two minutes in length, filmed in HD quality, and geo-tagged within the Shravani Mela area. Entries must not contain logos, watermarks, copyrighted material or AI-generated content. Submissions will be accepted through a Google Form available on the Bihar tourism department's website. The final winners will be selected based on a combination of jury evaluation and the performance of shortlisted videos on social media, including their reach, likes and shares over a 45-day period.

results.title: Sonam Wangchuk To March to Parliament As Hunger Strike Enters Day 20, Says ‘I’ll Come Back as a Ghost’ if It Fails (Video)
results.description: Climate activist and education reformer Sonam Wangchuk’s indefinite hunger strike entered its 20th consecutive day on Friday, with the campaigner reiterating his resolve to join a planned march to Parliament on July 20.
